description_long: This is a traditional Yixing-style teapot made from a rich, dark brown clay, featuring a rounded, faceted body with a short, stout spout and a robust, curved handle. The teapot's lid is topped with a sculpted animal figure, adding a decorative and cultural touch. The spout and handle both have ornate, dragon-like embellishments, enhancing the teapot's artistic appeal. Chinese calligraphy is inscribed on one side of the body, providing an elegant and authentic detail. The surface has a matte, slightly textured finish, typical of unglazed clay teapots. This object is compact and finely crafted, suitable for both functional use and decorative display.
description: A dark brown, ornately decorated Yixing-style teapot with a sculpted animal lid, dragon-motif handle and spout, and Chinese calligraphy on its faceted body.
description_view_0: Front view showing the spout with dragon motif, handle, animal-topped lid, and visible Chinese calligraphy.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the handle's ornate design and the animal figure on the lid, with no spout or calligraphy visible.
description_view_2: Side view displaying the handle and spout with dragon motifs, and a clear look at the teapot's faceted body.
description_view_3: Back view focusing on the handle attachment and the animal lid, with the spout and calligraphy not visible.
attribute_spout_orientation: The spout is short, slightly curved, and projects outward from the front of the teapot at a slight upward angle.
attribute_handle_design: The handle is a thick, rounded loop attached at the upper and lower rear of the teapot, featuring ornate dragon-like sculpting and ergonomic curvature.
attribute_lid_presence: Present; the lid is domed with a sculpted animal figure as a knob, fitting snugly into the teapot opening.
attribute_decorative_patterns: Chinese calligraphy on one side, dragon motifs on the spout and handle, and a sculpted animal figure on the lid; all in the same brown clay color.
